source_name: State and territory government net debt (Treasury budget papers, Pass 2C)
notes: Pass 2C value-entry update. 8 of 8 jurisdiction values now have official Treasury/Budget Paper values loaded. NSW, VIC, QLD, SA, TAS and ACT use general government sector net debt rows from 2025-26 Budget Paper material. WA remains Total Public Sector net debt (not GGS) and NT uses Non Financial Public Sector net debt, so both remain Partial coverage with explicit concept caveats. No national aggregate is published because rows are not safe to sum across different public-sector boundaries and jurisdictional scopes.
source_rights: CC BY 4.0 where stated by publisher; each state/territory Treasury copyright applies
citation: State and territory budget papers (NSW Budget Paper No. 1; VIC Statement of Finances; QLD Budget Paper No. 2; WA Budget Paper No. 3; SA Budget Statement; TAS Budget Paper No. 1; ACT Budget Outlook; NT Budget Paper 2).

rows.row_label: Net Debt ($m), Table 8.1 Key balance sheet aggregates of the general government sector (June 2026 Budget = $120,275m)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from NSW 2025-26 Budget Paper No. 1 (Budget Statement), Chapter 8 Balance Sheet, Table 8.1. General government sector net debt at 30 June 2026 = $120,275m = A$120.3 billion. Conceptually comparable to VIC and QLD GGS net debt rows; not directly comparable to WA which publishes Total Public Sector as its headline.

rows.row_label: Net debt $ billion, general government sector, 2025-26 budget (= 167.6)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from Victoria 2025-26 State Budget Strategy and Outlook (Budget Paper No. 2), Chapter 4 Fiscal Outlook table. General government sector net debt at 30 June 2026 = $167.6 billion. Conceptually comparable to NSW and QLD GGS net debt rows; not directly comparable to WA which publishes Total Public Sector as its headline.

rows.row_label: Net debt ($m), general government sector, 2025-26 Budget (= 41,803)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from QLD 2025-26 Budget Paper No. 2 (Budget Strategy and Outlook), Chapter 5 Balance Sheet and Cash Flows. General government sector net debt at 30 June 2026 = $41,803m = A$41.8 billion (text confirms "$22.092 billion" for 2024-25 estimated and the next column in the time-series row is 41,803 for 2025-26 Budget). Conceptually comparable to NSW and VIC GGS net debt rows.

rows.debt_concept: Total Public Sector net debt (WA Treasury headline measure; not General Government Sector)
rows.row_label: Total Public Sector Net Debt at 30 June ($m), Table 2 Key Budget Aggregates (= 38,968)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from WA 2025-26 Budget Paper No. 3 (Economic and Fiscal Outlook), Table 2 Key Budget Aggregates. WA publishes Total Public Sector net debt ($38,968m at 30 June 2026 = A$39.0 billion) as its headline because WA still owns major utilities (Water Corp, Synergy, ports) unlike eastern states that have privatised them. WA BP3 reports general-government net debt only in directional terms ("rises by $5.5 billion over four years to 2028-29") without a clean single-year GGS dollar figure in its overview tables. This row is therefore NOT directly comparable to NSW/VIC/QLD GGS rows above and is recorded as Partial coverage with the WA-specific concept name preserved.

rows.row_label: General government net debt ($m), Table 4.2 Key budget aggregates (= 25,201)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from South Australia 2025-26 Budget Statement, Budget Overview chapter, Table 4.2 Key budget aggregates. General government net debt for the 2025-26 Budget column = $25,201m = A$25.2 billion. Conceptually comparable to other GGS net debt rows, subject to jurisdiction-specific accounting and scope notes.

rows.row_label: Net Debt at 30 June, General Government Sector, Table 7.3 Fiscal indicators (= 7,081.1)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from Tasmania 2025-26 Budget Paper No. 1, Chapter 7 Estimated Outcome, Table 7.3 Fiscal indicators. General Government Sector Net Debt at 30 June for 2026 Budget = $7,081.1m = A$7.1 billion. The table also publishes a separate GFS Net Debt row ($5,969.7m); this dashboard uses the Net Debt row and does not treat the two concepts as identical.

rows.debt_concept: General government sector net debt excluding superannuation-related investments
rows.row_label: Net debt (excluding superannuation) ($m), General Government Sector Balance Sheet, Table 3.8.1 (= 11,001.8)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from ACT 2025-26 Budget Outlook, Financial Statements chapter, Table 3.8.1 General Government Sector Balance Sheet. Net debt excluding superannuation for the 2025-26 Budget column = $11,001.8m = A$11.0 billion. ACT's scope and scale differ from mainland states, and this row preserves the ACT-specific excluding-superannuation boundary.

rows.debt_concept: Non Financial Public Sector net debt (not General Government Sector)
rows.row_label: Non Financial Public Sector Net debt ($M), Table 1.1 Key Fiscal Indicators (= 12,191)
rows.comparability_note: Verified from Northern Territory 2025-26 Budget Paper No. 2, Budget Strategy and Outlook, Chapter 1 Fiscal Strategy and Outlook, Table 1.1 Key Fiscal Indicators. The published row is Non Financial Public Sector Net debt, 2025-26 Budget = $12,191m = A$12.2 billion. This is not General Government Sector net debt and must not be treated as identical to NSW/VIC/QLD/SA/TAS/ACT GGS rows.

fields.national_aggregate_note: No national aggregate is published. Although 8 of 8 jurisdictions now have official net-debt values loaded, summing them would be misleading because NSW/VIC/QLD/SA/TAS/ACT use GGS or ACT-specific GGS excluding-superannuation rows, while WA uses Total Public Sector net debt and NT uses Non Financial Public Sector net debt. Jurisdictional scope and public-sector ownership structures differ. Commonwealth AOFM debt is a separate measure on a separate card.
fields.comparability_basis: Preferred concept is general government sector net debt as published in each state/territory's latest Budget Paper / Statement for the 2025-26 budget cycle, usually a 30 June 2026 estimate. NSW, VIC, QLD, SA, TAS and ACT have official GGS or ACT-specific GGS net-debt rows loaded. WA publishes Total Public Sector as its headline measure because it still owns utilities; NT publishes Non Financial Public Sector net debt in its key fiscal indicators. WA and NT are therefore recorded as Partial coverage with the jurisdiction-specific concept names preserved. No total is calculated.
fields.audit_method: Pass 2C verified SA, TAS, ACT and NT from official Treasury/Budget Paper PDFs or official PDF text opened during this pass. Search terms included general government sector net debt, general government net debt, GGS net debt, GFS net debt and net debt. Values were entered only where the exact official row label, period and unit were visible in source text.
